checks.moreThanOneLine: You used more than one line of code within the function.

determineLeapYear.name: Determine if the year is a leap year
determineLeapYear.description: Write a function called isLeapYear that takes a year as its input and returns true if it is a leap year, or false if it is not.

solveInOneLine.name: Solve in one line of code
solveInOneLine.description: Can you solve this with only one line of code within the function?

year2015.name: Year 2015
year2015.description: 2015 is not divisible by 4 so is not a leap year.

year1970.name: Year 1970
year1970.description: 1970 is not divisible by 4 so is not a leap year.

year2100.name: Year 2100
year2100.description: 2100 is divisible by 100 but not by 400 so is not a leap year.

year1900.name: Year 1900
year1900.description: 1900 is divisible by 100 but not by 400 so is not a leap year.

year1800.name: Year 1800
year1800.description: 1800 is divisible by 100 but not by 400 so is not a leap year.

year2000.name: Year 2000
year2000.description: 2000 is divisible by 400 so is a leap year.

year2400.name: Year 2400
year2400.description: 2400 is divisible by 400 so is a leap year.

year1996.name: Year 1996
year1996.description: 1996 is divisible by 4 but not by 100 so is a leap year.

year1960.name: Year 1960
year1960.description: 1960 is divisible by 4 but not by 100 so is a leap year.

bonus1.name: One line of code
bonus1.description: Solve the exercise with only one line of code within the function.

basicRule.question: What's the basic rule for a leap year?
basicRule.answer: Years divisible by 4 are leap years. So `year % 4 === 0` is the starting test.

exceptions.question: Are there exceptions?
exceptions.answer: Yes. Years divisible by 100 are NOT leap years, even though they're divisible by 4. For example, 1900 isn't a leap year.

exceptionsToExceptions.question: Are there exceptions to the exception?
exceptionsToExceptions.answer: Yes. Years divisible by 400 ARE leap years after all. For example, 2000 is. So the rule cascades: divisible by 4, but not by 100, unless also by 400.

checkDivisibility.question: How do I check divisibility?
checkDivisibility.answer: Use the remainder operator `%`. `year % 4 === 0` is true when the year divides exactly by 4 with nothing left over. Same trick with 100 and 400.
